summary: No public page (/, /login, /signup, /pricing) links to /terms; the page is reachable only via a direct URL guess or one-directionally from /privacy-policy. Signup has no Terms/Privacy consent checkbox or link at all.
reproduction: 1. In a fresh unauthenticated browser session, navigate to https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/ (redirects to /dashboard, which bounces to /login).
2. Inspect all <a href> elements on /login — only /signup is present.
3. Navigate to /signup and inspect all <a href> elements and page text — only /login is present; no reference to 'terms' or 'privacy' anywhere in the signup form or its source.
4. Navigate to /pricing and inspect all <a href> elements — only /signup and /login are present, no /terms or /privacy-policy link anywhere on the page including footer.

5. Confirm /terms itself is reachable (200) only by typing the URL directly, or by following the one link to it that exists inside /privacy-policy (a one-directional reference, not a site-wide footer/nav).
6. Repeated in a second fresh browser session with identical results.
expected: A product with paid subscriptions, GST tax invoicing, and account cancellation/refund clauses should surface a discoverable link to its Terms & Conditions at minimum from the signup flow (ideally as an explicit consent checkbox) and from a shared footer/nav on public pages, so users can find and be presented with the terms they are bound by.
observed: Zero links to /terms exist anywhere in /, /login, /signup, or /pricing (confirmed via full DOM anchor enumeration in two independent sessions). /signup contains no terms/privacy reference or consent checkbox at all. The only inbound link to /terms in the entire public surface is a single reference from /privacy-policy.

summary: Live production /terms page contains unfilled bracketed placeholder text in §5 (Subscription Plans & Pricing) and §9 (Refunds) — '[Operator ABN]', '[Business Name]', and a full placeholder sentence for the refund policy — reachable by any real visitor, which is placeholder content on a legal document even though CLM-098 claims the page is free of placeholder content.
reproduction: 1. Navigate to https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/terms unauthenticated.
2. Scroll to section '5. Subscription Plans & Pricing' — read the paragraph beginning 'Tax invoices require a registered Australian Business Number.'
3. Observe literal bracketed text: '[Operator ABN]' and '[Business Name]' rendered as bold placeholders, not real values.

4. Scroll to section '9. Refunds' — observe the final sentence is a literal bracketed instruction to the operator: '[Operator: state your refund policy here — e.g., pro-rated refunds within X days, or no refunds — before relying on this page as final.]'
5. Confirmed present in server-rendered HTML (curl, no JS) as well as in the browser DOM.
6. Repeated in a second fresh browser session with identical results (string-matched via page.evaluate).
expected: Per CLM-098, the legal-page-honesty fix should mean terms content contains no placeholder/lorem-ipsum-style text reachable by users.
observed: Two of eighteen sections contain live, unfilled bracket placeholders visible to every visitor, including for tax-invoice ABN/Business Name details and the refund policy itself — exactly the kind of unfinished-template content a paying customer relying on a Refunds clause or tax invoice would notice.

summary: Terms §18 (Contact) and §5/§9 direct users to reach the operator 'via the Settings page or the in-app support channel' for questions, tax-invoice details, refunds, and account closure — but no such contact mechanism (email, contact form, chat widget, or support link) exists anywhere in the product, including on the Settings page and dashboard.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123 and navigate to /dashboard/settings.
2. Enumerate every <a> and <button> element's text on the page — none reference 'support', 'contact', or 'help'.
3. Enumerate every <a> and <button> element's text on /dashboard (main shell) — same result, none present.
4. Search the full frontend source for mailto: links, support/contact emails, or chat-widget scripts (Intercom/Crisp/Zendesk/etc.) — none exist anywhere in the codebase.
5. Search the whole frontend for the literal phrase 'in-app support channel' — it appears ONLY inside the terms and privacy-policy legal copy itself, never as an actual implemented feature.

6. Read Settings > Privacy tab text: it independently says 'contact us to request a full data export or deletion' with no link, email, or button — corroborating that 'contact us' is prose only, not a functioning channel anywhere in the app.
expected: A Terms clause that tells users to reach the operator via 'the Settings page or the in-app support channel' implies a real, discoverable contact mechanism exists at that location.
observed: No contact mechanism of any kind (email, form, chat, ticket link) exists on Settings, the dashboard shell, or anywhere else in the product. A user wanting to request a refund (§9), close their account (§15), or supply GST tax-invoice details (§5) has no actual path to do so despite the Terms directing them to one.

summary: Terms document mixes currencies and jurisdictions without reconciliation: pricing/GST/tax-invoice clauses (§5) are entirely AUD/Australia-specific, but the liability cap in §14 is denominated in USD ('fifty U.S. dollars ($50)') and §17 Governing Law specifies 'the laws of the State of Delaware, USA' with no mention of Australia at all — internally inconsistent for a product whose entire commercial framework (live pricing API, GST math) is Australian.
reproduction: 1. Navigate to /terms, read §5 Subscription Plans & Pricing: 'priced in Australian dollars (AUD) and inclusive of the 10% Goods and Services Tax (GST)'; cross-checked live against GET /api/billing/plans which returns currency: 'AUD', gstIncluded: true, matching prices exactly.
2. Read §14 Disclaimers & Limitation of Liability: liability cap stated as 'fifty U.S. dollars ($50)' for free-tier users — a different currency than every other monetary figure in the document.

3. Read §17 Governing Law: 'governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, USA' — no reference to Australian law or an Australian entity anywhere in the document, despite ABN/GST tax-invoice obligations being referenced in §5.
4. Reproduced identically in a second fresh session (governingLawText captured verbatim both times).
expected: A single legal document's monetary and jurisdictional clauses should be internally consistent, or the inconsistency should be explained (e.g., a US-incorporated entity operating an AU-facing product would typically still note this explicitly).
observed: The document reads as a US SaaS Terms template only partially localized for the AUD/GST/ABN business reality described in its own §5 — the $50 USD cap and Delaware governing law were not updated to match.

summary: No wireframe exists for /terms (SCREEN-MATRIX.json coverage_gap: 'route-without-wireframe'), so item 1 of the §3.2 protocol (visual conformance vs wireframe) could not be performed against a design source of truth; visual/content review was instead performed against product-sense expectations for a SaaS legal page, cross-checked against live billing behavior.
reproduction: 1. Checked design/screens/ and SCREEN-MATRIX.json — wireframe_files: [] for screen_id 'terms'.
2. Performed full-page desktop (1440x900) and mobile (390x844) screenshots and manual content review in place of wireframe comparison.
expected: N/A — documented per matrix; not a defect in the product itself.
observed: Confirmed no wireframe artifact exists; visual conformance instead assessed against reasonable product expectations (see report body).
